records.ety: [L. ingeniosus, fr. ingenium innate or natural quality, natural capacity, genius: cf. F. ingnieux. See Engine.]

defs: Possessed of genius, or the faculty of invention; skillful or promp to invent; having an aptitude to contrive, or to form new combinations; as, an ingenious author, mechanic.
quotes: A man . . . very wise and ingenious in feats of war.
Thou, king, send out For torturers ingenious.
The more ingenious men are, the more apt are they to trouble themselves.

defs: Proceeding from, pertaining to, or characterized by, genius or ingenuity; of curious design, structure, or mechanism; as, an ingenious model, or machine; an ingenious scheme, contrivance, etc.
quotes: Thus men go wrong with an ingenious skill.

defs: Witty; shrewd; adroit; keen; sagacious; as, an ingenious reply.

quotes: A course of learning and ingenious studies.
